genetic_lineage.overview: Greco appears indigenous to southern Italy despite its name suggesting Greek origins. DNA studies have not confirmed any direct relationship to Greek varieties.

parents.name: Unknown
parents.notes: No parentage established. Likely indigenous to Campania despite Greek-derived name.

siblings.name: Greco Bianco di Gerace
siblings.notes: Calabrian variety sometimes confused with Greco di Tufo but DNA shows they are distinct.

genetic_lineage.genetic_significance: Greco's Italian origin despite its Greek name demonstrates how grape names can mislead about variety origins. The variety is genetically distinct from Greek varieties despite historical assumptions.

clones.name: Clone ISV-Gr1
clones.characteristics: Standard certified clone with good aromatic development and mineral character.

clones.name: Clone ISV-Gr2
clones.characteristics: Selected for higher acidity retention and aging potential.

clones.name: Tufo Massal Selection
clones.characteristics: Traditional selections from volcanic Tufo soils. Maximum mineral intensity with exceptional aging potential.

clones.name: Clone R3
clones.characteristics: More productive clone suitable for everyday wines while maintaining varietal character.

terroir.effect: The defining soil of Greco di Tufo. Porous volcanic rock provides excellent drainage and imparts distinctive mineral complexity. Produces the most age-worthy wines.

wine_character.aromatics: Citrus, almond, volcanic mineral, honey with age

terroir.effect: Found in other Campanian sites. Produces rounder, more approachable wines with less mineral intensity.
Well-drained soils producing more aromatic, earlier-drinking styles.

resistances.notes: Performs well in dry Campanian summers.

disease_profile.general_notes: Greco is a mid-ripening variety that develops high sugar levels while maintaining acidity. The variety's thick skins contribute to structure and aging potential. Wines can be austere when young, requiring time to show their complexity. Best sites are volcanic soils at moderate elevation.

regional_expressions.style_summary: Campania's finest white wine appellation, producing structured, mineral wines from volcanic soils. The best examples can age for decades, developing remarkable complexity with honey, almond, and petrol notes.
Broader Campanian appellation producing Greco in slightly softer style than Tufo. Good value alternative to the DOCG wines.
Regional designation allowing more flexibility. Quality varies but some producers use it for innovative styles including skin-contact and barrel-aged versions.
